_README: MASSALIA hoplite mercenary daily-decision cards. 5 contracts x 10 cards = 50. While a hoplite is on a contract, his daily routine pool is REPLACED by the matching 'pool' set below (instead of the home 'citizen' pool). Schema matches content/routines/routines.json card shape: id, pool, label, scene, tags, effects[], optional classMods. Effect types in use: change_stat (militia|prestige|intelligence|drachmae-no use change_drachmae), change_drachmae, change_composure, change_trait (op: add), change_party_favor. Risk/injury/death are resolved by the contract engine on the season tick, NOT inside these cards (cards are the daily texture; the engine rolls lethality). Some cards carry small risk flavor via composure/trait only.

pools.merc-trade-ship: Trade-ship guard (coastal escort, 1 season). Theme: cramped decks, pirates, salt, boredom, small loyalties.
pools.merc-gaul-caravan: Caravan escort, Gaulish interior (1 season). Theme: forests, Celtic chiefs, river fords, strange rites, hard marches.
pools.merc-syracuse: Syracusan service vs Sicilian bandits (2 seasons). Theme: tyrant's pay, hill ambushes, Greek-on-Greek, glory and politics.
pools.merc-carthage: Carthaginian campaign, South Iberia (2 seasons). Theme: silver mines, Punic officers, polyglot army, brutal sieges, plunder.
pools.merc-ptolemy: Ptolemaic war vs the Seleucids (4 seasons). Theme: vast royal army, Egypt's wealth, phalanx clashes, disease, legend or death.

cards.label: Stand the night watch
cards.scene: The sea is black glass and every creak could be oars. You hold the rail till dawn.

cards.label: Drill the deckhands
cards.scene: Frightened sailors are not soldiers, but a spear in hand steadies a man. You teach them the wall.

cards.label: Repel the pirate boarders
cards.scene: A low hull comes out of the haze, grapnels flying. This is the work you are paid for.

cards.label: Claim your share of the cargo
cards.scene: The captain is grateful and the hold is full. A guard's cut is custom, if you press for it.

cards.label: Ride out the squall
cards.scene: The mast groans, the deck heaves, men pray to gods they'd forgotten. You bail and hold on.

cards.label: Dice with the crew below deck
cards.scene: Knucklebones rattle on a barrel-head. A guard who wins is feared; one who loses is fleeced.

cards.label: Shore leave at a strange port
cards.scene: Wine you don't know the name of, a tongue you half-follow, a night you'll half-remember.

cards.label: Mend your kit and rest
cards.scene: Oil the bronze, whip the fraying straps, sleep while you can. The sea will want you again.

cards.label: Win the merchant's trust
cards.scene: He is rich, well-connected back in the Greek ports, and watching how you carry yourself.

cards.label: Spare the beaten pirates
cards.scene: They kneel on the wet boards. The captain wants them over the side. The choice is yours to argue.

cards.label: March the column through the forest
cards.scene: Oak-dark and endless, the track barely a track. You watch the treeline and count heads.

cards.label: Force the river ford
cards.scene: Cold water to the chest, mules balking, the far bank a good place for an ambush. You go first.

cards.label: Drink with the Gaulish chief
cards.scene: He pours you beer the size of a helmet and roars a toast. Refuse and you insult him; match him and you're his.

cards.label: Break the hillmen's ambush
cards.scene: Slings and spears from the rocks above. You turn the column and drive uphill into them.

cards.label: Witness the grove rite
cards.scene: Mistletoe, a white bull, druids in the dusk. It is not your god, but power is power.

cards.label: Trade for amber and furs
cards.scene: Your pay buys cheap here what sells dear in Massalia. A soldier with an eye for cargo dies richer.

cards.label: Learn the Celtic tongue
cards.scene: Word by word around the fire. A man who can speak to the locals is worth three who can't.

cards.label: Make camp and rest the column
cards.scene: Fires banked low, sentries set, the forest breathing around you. Sleep is a weapon too.

cards.label: Stay out of the clan feud
cards.scene: Two villages, an old killing, and the caravan caught between. The wise guard guards the cargo, not the grudge.

cards.label: Answer the champion's challenge
cards.scene: A naked Gaul with a long sword steps out and points at you, grinning. The whole column watches.

cards.label: Muster with the tyrant's companies
cards.scene: Syracuse pays well and parades hard. You take your place among soldiers from a dozen cities.

cards.label: Hunt bandits in the hills
cards.scene: They know every goat-path; you know the phalanx. The trick is making them stand and fight.

cards.label: Clear the bandit-held village
cards.scene: Greek faces on the other side of the spears. Pay does not ask whose blood it buys.

cards.label: Attend the tyrant's court
cards.scene: Marble, flatterers, and a ruler who remembers useful men. You are seen, and you make sure of it.

cards.label: Divide the bandit hoard
cards.scene: Years of stolen silver in a cave. The officers take theirs; a hard man takes his before they do.

cards.label: Spare the bandits' families
cards.scene: Women and children behind the last wall. The order is to leave nothing. Your spear, your conscience.

cards.label: Out-soldier the rival captains
cards.scene: Mercenary glory is a zero-sum thing. Outshine the others and the tyrant's gold finds you.

cards.label: Sit with the wounded
cards.scene: Men you led, broken on the cots. You owe them your face, even when it costs you.

cards.label: Feast in the soldiers' camp
cards.scene: Sicilian wine, roast pig, songs in every dialect of the Greek world. For a night, no one is a hireling.

cards.label: Tend your gear and your nerves
cards.scene: Two seasons is a long war. You keep the bronze bright and the mind quiet, one day at a time.

cards.label: Take the Punic officer's coin
cards.scene: Carthage hires the world. You stand in a line of Iberians, Libyans, Balearic slingers, and Greeks like you.

cards.label: Guard the silver mines
cards.scene: The richest holes in the world, worked by slaves, coveted by every tribe. You stand between them and the silver.

cards.label: Storm the Iberian hillfort
cards.scene: Ladders against red walls, stones and fire coming down. Carthage spends mercenaries first.

cards.label: Sack the fallen town
cards.scene: The walls are breached and the law is gone for an afternoon. Take what you will; live with it after.

cards.label: Hold your men back from the worst
cards.scene: A burning town does not need more cruelty. You keep your spears in hand and your name clean.

cards.label: Bridge the army's tongues
cards.scene: Iberian, Punic, Greek, Gaulish — an officer who can make them one line is worth his weight in mine-silver.

cards.label: Impress the Barcid commander
cards.scene: A great Carthaginian house leads here. To be remembered by such men is worth more than a season's pay.

cards.label: Survive the camp fever
cards.scene: More mercenaries die of the flux than of swords. You drink boiled water, burn the dead, and endure.

cards.label: Demand the wages owed
cards.scene: Carthage is rich and slow to pay. Mercenaries who don't press get nothing — and ones who press too hard get worse.

cards.label: Rest in the Iberian camp
cards.scene: Strange stars, foreign fires, the long ache of a campaign that won't end. You husband your strength.

cards.label: Take your place in the royal phalanx
cards.scene: Egypt's army stretches past seeing — pike-blocks, elephants, gold-chased standards. You are one bronze speck in a king's war.

cards.label: Hold the line against the Seleukids
cards.scene: Sarissa to sarissa, two royal phalanxes grinding in the dust. Hold, push, breathe, hold. This is the great war of the age.

cards.label: Stand against the war-elephants
cards.scene: The earth shakes and the line wavers. A man who does not run when the beasts come is a man remembered.

cards.label: Claim a share of royal plunder
cards.scene: When a king wins, he pays kingly. Captured baggage, Seleukid silver, the spoils of a satrapy.

cards.label: Be noticed at Alexandria
cards.scene: The greatest city in the world, and you in it — a Massaliote spear the Ptolemies might just remember.

cards.label: Endure the marsh sickness
cards.scene: The Delta breeds fevers that kill more than the Seleukids. You bury friends and keep marching.

cards.label: Rally the breaking wing
cards.scene: The flank buckles, men look for somewhere to run. You step into the gap and give them a voice to follow.

cards.label: Take the surrender honourably
cards.scene: A Seleukid company throws down its pikes. You could cut them apart for sport. You take their oath instead.

cards.label: Walk the wonders of Egypt
cards.scene: Pyramids older than memory, a library of all the world's words. A season here marks a man for life.

cards.label: Survive another day of the long war
cards.scene: Four seasons is an age in a king's war. You keep your edge, your wits, and your name — and you live to carry the gold home.
